title: Klaviyo vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Klaviyo and Drip email marketing platforms. See pricing, features, and which tool wins for ecommerce businesses.
h1: Klaviyo vs Drip: Detailed Feature and Pricing Comparison
intro: Klaviyo and Drip are both powerful email marketing platforms designed specifically for ecommerce businesses. Both offer automation, segmentation, and SMS capabilities, but they differ significantly in pricing models, feature depth, and integration approaches. This comparison will help you determine which platform best fits your business needs and budget.

comparison_table.tool_a: Freemium (free up to 500 subscribers, then from $45/month)
comparison_table.tool_b: Paid only (starts at $39/month, no free tier)
Deep ecommerce store integration and revenue tracking
Revenue attribution and visual automation builder

choose_a_if: You're starting out and want to test with a free plan before paying
You need advanced predictive analytics and AI-powered recommendations
You prioritize platform ratings and customer satisfaction scores
choose_b_if: You want a dedicated solution with no free tier complexity
Revenue attribution and precise ROI tracking are critical to your strategy
You're interested in earning affiliate commissions by recommending the tool

faq.a: Klaviyo edges ahead with higher customer ratings (4.6 vs 4.4 on G2), a free tier for startups, and predictive analytics capabilities. However, 'better' depends on your needs—Drip excels at revenue attribution and may suit businesses prioritizing detailed ecommerce tracking. For most users, Klaviyo offers more flexibility and features at comparable price points.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Klaviyo or Drip?
faq.a: Drip has a lower starting price at $39/month with no free option, while Klaviyo starts at $45/month but includes a free tier up to 500 subscribers. For small businesses under 500 contacts, Klaviyo's free plan makes it significantly cheaper. At larger scales, pricing becomes comparable, though the total cost depends on your subscriber count and feature needs.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to Klaviyo?
faq.a: Yes, switching is possible. Both platforms support CSV imports and integrations that allow you to migrate your subscriber lists and contact data. However, automation workflows and campaign history typically need to be manually recreated. Most migrations can be completed in a few hours to days, depending on complexity.
